questions.text: Production of goods in large quantities after processing from raw materials to more valuable products is called:
questions.explanation: Manufacturing is the process of converting raw materials into finished goods on a large scale.

questions.text: Manufacturing sector is considered the backbone of development because:
options: It creates jobs in secondary and tertiary sectors
It brings in foreign exchange through exports
questions.explanation: Manufacturing modernizes agriculture and is the main source of employment and economic strength.

questions.text: The contribution of the manufacturing sector to India's GDP has stagnated around ______ percent over the last two decades.
questions.explanation: Out of a total of 27% for industry, manufacturing accounts for 17%, which is lower than some East Asian economies.

questions.text: What does NMCC stand for in the context of Indian industry?
questions.explanation: NMCC was set up with the objective of improving the productivity and growth of the manufacturing sector.

questions.text: Which of the following factors does NOT influence the location of an industry?
questions.explanation: Industrial location is determined by economic factors like land, labor, capital, power, and market.

questions.text: Many industries tend to come together to make use of the advantages offered by the urban centers known as:
questions.explanation: Agglomeration economies occur when businesses benefit from being located near one another in a city.

questions.text: Industries that use agricultural raw materials like cotton, wool, and jute are classified as:
questions.explanation: Agro-based industries derive their primary raw materials from agricultural products.

questions.text: Basic or Key industries are those which:
options: Produce goods for direct consumer use
Supply their products as raw materials to other industries
questions.explanation: Iron and steel is a basic industry because its products are used by almost all other industries.

questions.text: A small-scale industry is defined with reference to the maximum investment allowed on the ________ of a unit.
questions.explanation: Small-scale industry classification is based on the capital investment limit, which has changed over time.

questions.text: Industries owned and operated by government agencies like BHEL or SAIL are called:
questions.explanation: Public sector industries are owned and managed by the state or central government.

questions.text: Which type of industry is owned and operated by a group of people who are the producers of raw materials?
questions.explanation: The sugar industry in Maharashtra and the coir industry in Kerala are examples of the cooperative sector.

questions.text: The first successful textile mill was established in ______ in 1854.
questions.explanation: Mumbai provided a port for export, availability of raw cotton, and a humid climate suitable for the mill.

questions.text: India exports yarn to which of the following countries?
questions.explanation: Japan is a major importer of Indian cotton yarn, though India also exports to many other nations.

questions.text: India is the largest producer of raw jute and jute goods and stands at second place as an exporter after ______.
questions.explanation: Bangladesh is India's main competitor in the global jute market.

questions.text: Most of the jute mills in India are located along the banks of which river?
questions.explanation: The Hugli basin in West Bengal provides water, cheap transport, and proximity to jute-growing areas.

questions.text: India stands second as a world producer of sugar but occupies the first place in the production of:
questions.explanation: India is the world leader in traditional forms of sugar production.

questions.text: Why is the sugar industry shifting from North India to South and West India?
options: Cooler climate allows longer crushing season
questions.explanation: Southern and Western states like Maharashtra have better climatic and organizational conditions for sugar production.

questions.text: Iron and steel industry is called a 'Basic Industry' because:
options: All other industries depend on it for machinery
It uses only iron as raw material
questions.explanation: Steel is needed for engineering goods, construction material, defense, and medical equipment.

questions.text: What is the correct ratio of Iron ore, Coking coal, and Limestone required to manufacture steel?
questions.explanation: 4 tons of iron ore, 2 tons of coking coal, and 1 ton of limestone are roughly needed to produce steel.

questions.text: Which region in India has the maximum concentration of iron and steel industries?
questions.explanation: The Chhotanagpur region has low-cost iron ore, high-grade raw materials, and cheap labor.

questions.text: ______ is the second most important metallurgical industry in India.
questions.explanation: Aluminum is popular because it is light, corrosion-resistant, and a good conductor of heat.

questions.text: Which mineral is used as the raw material in aluminum smelters?
questions.explanation: Bauxite is a very bulky, dark reddish-colored rock used to extract alumina.

questions.text: The chemical industry in India contributes approximately ______ percent of the GDP.
questions.explanation: The chemical industry is large and diverse, comprising both organic and inorganic sectors.

questions.text: The fertilizer industry is centered around the production of:
questions.explanation: India is the third-largest producer of nitrogenous fertilizers.

questions.text: Which industry requires bulky raw materials like limestone, silica, and gypsum?
questions.explanation: Cement is essential for construction activity; the first plant was set up in Chennai in 1904.

questions.text: Which city is known as the 'Electronic Capital' of India?
questions.explanation: Bengaluru is the hub for India's booming IT and electronics industry.

questions.text: Which of the following is an effect of industrial air pollution?
questions.explanation: High proportions of undesirable gases like sulphur dioxide and carbon monoxide cause severe environmental and health issues.

questions.text: Water pollution by industries is primarily caused by:
options: Organic and inorganic industrial wastes discharged into rivers
questions.explanation: Paper, chemical, and textile industries are major culprits in discharging untreated effluents.

questions.text: Thermal pollution occurs when:
options: Hot water from factories is drained into rivers before cooling
Solar panels reflect too much heat
questions.explanation: Thermal pollution harms aquatic life by suddenly changing the water temperature.

questions.text: How many liters of fresh water can be polluted by one liter of industrial waste?
questions.explanation: Industrial effluents have a massive impact on fresh water resources.

questions.text: Which of the following is a method to conserve water in industries?
questions.explanation: Industries can reduce their environmental footprint by adopting efficient water management practices.

questions.text: Primary treatment of industrial effluents involves:
questions.explanation: Primary treatment is the first stage which physically removes solids from wastewater.

questions.text: Secondary treatment of industrial effluents is primarily ______ in nature.
questions.explanation: Secondary treatment uses microbes to break down organic matter in the waste.

questions.text: NTPC is a major power providing corporation in India which has ISO certification for:
questions.explanation: NTPC follows a proactive approach for preserving the natural environment and resources.

questions.text: Air pollution can be reduced by using ______ instead of coal in factories.
questions.explanation: Oil and gas produce fewer particulate emissions compared to coal.

questions.text: The software technology parks of India provide ______ to software experts.
options: Single window service and high speed data communication
questions.explanation: Software parks act as hubs that support the growth of the IT industry.

questions.text: Which of the following is an inorganic chemical?
questions.explanation: Inorganic chemicals include sulphuric acid, nitric acid, alkalies, and soda ash.

questions.text: The fertilizer industry has expanded significantly after the:
questions.explanation: The Green Revolution increased the demand for nitrogenous and complex fertilizers to improve crop yields.

questions.text: Noise pollution can cause:
questions.explanation: Industrial and construction activities contribute significantly to high noise levels, affecting health.

questions.text: Particulate matter in the air can be reduced by fitting ______ to factory stacks.
questions.explanation: These devices trap smoke and dust particles before they are released into the atmosphere.
